chunks.text: ACM SIGCHI elected Professor Eric Paulos to the ACM CHI Academy, an honorary group recognizing individuals who have made substantial contributions to human-computer interaction and shaped the discipline. Paulos is one of eleven inductees this year. The CHI Academy honors work advancing research and innovation in interactive technologies and the broader HCI field.

Eric Paulos is recognized for exploring how people interact with networked devices, sensing systems, and urban technologies. His research spans human-computer interaction, design, and emerging technologies, often focusing on how computing supports civic engagement and everyday life. The page also categorizes the item under Faculty, Honors, and People, and links to the 2026 ACM SIGCHI awards and special recognition.
